lines.gurmukhi: ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਸੋਭਾ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਰੇ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: grihi sobhaa jaa kai re naeh |
lines.english: When someone's household has no glory,
Gond:
Deep within, there is no contentment.
Without his bride, the wealth of Maya, he suffers in pain. ||1||
the guests who come there depart still hungry.
So praise this bride, which can shake the consciousness
Of even the most dedicated ascetics and sages. ||1||Pause||
Abandoning the Lord's servant, she sleeps with the world.
This bride is the daughter of a wretched miser.
Standing at the door of the holy man,
she says, "I have come to your sanctuary; now save me!" ||2||
This bride is so beautiful.
The bells on her ankles make soft music.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਲਗੁ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਤਊ ਲਗੁ ਸੰਗੇ ॥
lines.translit: jau lag praan taoo lag sange |
lines.english: As long as there is the breath of life in the man, she remains attached to him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਹਿ ਤ ਚਲੀ ਬੇਗਿ ਉਠਿ ਨੰਗੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naeh ta chalee beg utth nange |3|
lines.english: But when it is no more, she quickly gets up and departs, bare-footed. ||3||
This bride has conquered the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਸ ਅਠ ਪੁਰਾਣ ਤੀਰਥ ਰਸ ਕੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: das atth puraan teerath ras keea |
lines.english: The eighteen Puraanas and the sacred shrines of pilgrimage love her as well.
This bride has no restraint or limits.
She destroyed the great emperors and kings of the world. ||4||
She pierced the hearts of Brahma, Shiva and Vishnu.
When the clay pot of these five passions bursts,
She is in collusion with the five thieving passions.
then, says Kabeer, by Guru's Mercy, one is released. ||5||5||8||
just so, without the Naam, the Name of the Lord, how can anyone be carried across?
Gond:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੰਦਰ ਮਹਿ ਬਲਹਰ ਨਾ ਠਾਹਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaise mandar meh balahar naa tthaaharai |
lines.english: As the house will not stand when the supporting beams are removed from within it,
Without the pitcher, the water is not contained;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਰਉ ਤਿਸੈ ਜੁ ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaarau tisai ju raam na chetai |
lines.english: One who does not remember the Lord - let him burn;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨ ਮਨ ਰਮਤ ਰਹੈ ਮਹਿ ਖੇਤੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tan man ramat rahai meh khetai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: his body and mind have remained absorbed in this field of the world. ||1||Pause||
just so, without the Holy Saint, the mortal departs in misery. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਹਲਹਰ ਬਿਨਾ ਜਿਮੀ ਨਹੀ ਬੋਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: jaise halahar binaa jimee nahee boeeai |
lines.english: Without a farmer, the land is not planted;
without a thread, how can the beads be strung?
Without a loop, how can the knot be tied?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਬਾਲੁ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jaise maat pitaa bin baal na hoee |
lines.english: Without a mother or father there is no child;
Just so, without the Holy Saint, the mortal departs in misery. ||2||
Without a horse, how can there be a rider?
Without the Holy Saint, one cannot reach the Court of the Lord. ||3||
just so, without water, how can the clothes be washed?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਜੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਹੀ ਲੀਜੈ ਫੇਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: jaise baaje bin nahee leejai feree |
lines.english: Just as without music, there is no dancing,
the bride rejected by her husband is dishonored.
Says Kabeer, do this one thing:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਕੂਟੈ ਤਉ ਜਮ ਤੇ ਛੂਟੈ ॥
lines.translit: man koottai tau jam te chhoottai |
lines.english: Pounding down his mind, he escapes from the Messenger of Death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂਟਨੁ ਸੋਇ ਜੁ ਮਨ ਕਉ ਕੂਟੈ ॥
lines.translit: koottan soe ju man kau koottai |
lines.english: He alone is a pimp, who pounds down his mind.
Gond:
become Gurmukh, and you shall never die again. ||4||6||9||
such a pimp attains total liberation. ||1||
Pounding and beating his mind, he puts it to the test;
Who is called a pimp in this world?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਚਨੁ ਸੋਇ ਜੁ ਮਨ ਸਿਉ ਨਾਚੈ ॥
lines.translit: naachan soe ju man siau naachai |
lines.english: He alone is a dancer, who dances with his mind.
The Lord is not satisfied with falsehood; He is pleased only with Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਬੋਲਨ ਕੇ ਮਾਹਿ ਬੀਚਾਰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sagal bolan ke maeh beechaar |1| rahaau |
lines.english: In all speech, one must carefully consider. ||1||Pause||
The Lord is the Protector of the dancer with such a mind. ||2||
So play the beat of the drum in the mind.
She alone is a street-dancer, who cleanses her body-street,
She who embraces devotional worship for the Lord
- I accept such a street-dancer as my Guru. ||3||
and educates the five passions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਸਕਰੁ ਸੋਇ ਜਿ ਤਾਤਿ ਨ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: tasakar soe ji taat na karai |
lines.english: He alone is a thief, who is above envy,
and who uses his sense organs to chant the Lord's Name.
Says Kabeer, these are the qualities of the one
I know as my Blessed Divine Guru, who is the most beautiful and wise. ||4||7||10||
